cases.title: Disambiguate a common member name
cases.prompt: Find all usages of the `date` property on `EventBean`, not every `date` symbol in the repo. List each call site with file path and line number.
cases.expected_output: A usage list scoped to the exact member identity.
expectations: Resolves the member with containingType or fileHint before scanning usages
Scopes the result to the requested Kotlin member instead of unrelated `date` symbols
Does not use raw text search as the primary identity mechanism

source.summary: Adapted from the value-proof member disambiguation case

cases.title: Disambiguate callers of a common function name
cases.prompt: Find callers of `StandaloneAnalysisBackend.handle()` rather than every `handle()` function in the project. Show the call hierarchy rooted at the exact function.
cases.expected_output: A call hierarchy rooted at the exact function identity.
expectations: Disambiguates the function using containingType, kind, or fileHint
Does not silently pick one of multiple candidates without disambiguation
Reports callers specific to the target class

source.summary: Adapted from the value-proof caller disambiguation case

cases.title: Prove exhaustive cross-workspace references
cases.prompt: Find every reference to `AnalysisBackend` across the entire workspace and state whether the search was exhaustive or truncated.
cases.expected_output: A complete reference report with explicit search completeness metadata.
expectations: Reports completeness metadata for the reference search
Lists references grouped by file
Does not claim completeness without evidence from the tool

source.summary: Adapted from the value-proof exhaustive references case

cases.title: Trace every sealed hierarchy implementation
cases.prompt: List every implementation of the sealed interface `AnalysisBackend`. For each implementation, show its file location and module.
cases.expected_output: A semantic subtype report with module attribution.
expectations: Uses semantic resolution instead of text search
Lists all sealed subtypes with file paths
Identifies which module each implementation lives in

source.summary: Adapted from the value-proof sealed hierarchy case

cases.title: Safely rename a symbol across files
cases.prompt: Rename `resolveKastCliPath` to `locateKastCliPath` across the workspace. Show the edit plan before applying and confirm the workspace is still clean afterward.
cases.expected_output: A multi-file rename plan and validated post-edit status.
expectations: Uses the semantic rename path instead of find-and-replace
Shows an edit plan before applying
Reports clean diagnostics after the rename

source.summary: Adapted from the value-proof rename case

cases.title: Edit and validate atomically
cases.prompt: Add a `@Deprecated("Use locateKastCliPath instead")` annotation to the `resolveKastCliPath` declaration and confirm the file still compiles after the edit.
cases.expected_output: A validated edit report with diagnostics evidence.
expectations: Uses `kast_write_and_validate` rather than a raw edit tool
Validates the edit atomically
Does not claim success without reporting compile cleanliness

source.summary: Adapted from the value-proof atomic edit case

cases.title: Summarize a large class structurally
cases.prompt: Summarize the public API of `CliCommandCatalog` and list every public type, enum, and data class it contains with their member signatures.
cases.expected_output: A scaffold-rooted API summary for a large class.
expectations: Uses `kast_scaffold` as the primary information source
Lists nested types accurately
Does not hallucinate members that do not exist

source.summary: Adapted from the value-proof scaffold case

cases.title: Discover modules in one semantic call
cases.prompt: List every module in this workspace and how many Kotlin source files each contains.
cases.expected_output: A module inventory with Kotlin source file counts.
expectations: Uses `kast_workspace_files` instead of recursive directory traversal
Reports module names and file counts
Completes without iterative file-system crawling

source.summary: Adapted from the value-proof workspace discovery case

cases.title: Trace two-level deletion impact
cases.prompt: I want to delete `CliService.readFile()`. Show every direct caller, then for each caller show its callers at depth 2 and note which ones live in test files.
cases.expected_output: A bounded depth-2 caller impact report with production and test classification.
expectations: Resolves the exact function before tracing callers
Shows a depth-2 call hierarchy
Distinguishes test files from production files

source.summary: Adapted from the value-proof impact analysis case

cases.title: Trace cross-module symbol flow
cases.prompt: Trace how `AnalysisBackend` flows from its declaration to its consumers in other modules and show the file-to-file dependency chain.
cases.expected_output: A concrete file-to-file flow report across module boundaries.
expectations: Uses semantic analysis rather than grep
Identifies consumers in at least one other module
Shows concrete file-to-file relationships

source.summary: Adapted from the value-proof cross-module flow case

cases.title: Understand a Kotlin file semantically
cases.prompt: Help me understand this Kotlin file and how it is wired into the rest of the project.
cases.expected_output: A semantic summary rooted in the Kotlin file and its call graph.
expectations: Starts with native Kast semantic tools
Uses the scaffold and follow-up semantic tracing rather than raw file reads
Avoids generic text search for Kotlin identity

cases.title: Find Kotlin usages semantically
cases.prompt: Where is this Kotlin symbol used, and what code depends on it?
cases.expected_output: A reference report for the exact symbol and its callers.
expectations: Resolves the symbol before finding usages
Uses semantic reference and caller tools
Does not fall back to grep or rg

cases.title: Recover from missing Kast bootstrap
cases.prompt: I tried using Kast and the first command failed because `KAST_CLI_PATH` is empty. Bootstrap Kast for this workspace and keep tracing the Kotlin symbol semantically.
cases.expected_output: A successful bootstrap followed by semantic tracing with Kast.
expectations: Recovers by using the Kast bootstrap path
Keeps the workflow in native Kast tools afterward
Does not abandon the semantic workflow

source.summary: Bootstrap recovery route

cases.title: Stay productive when the skill is already selected
cases.prompt: The Kast skill is already selected for a Kotlin flow-tracing task. Start with the first useful semantic command instead of reading maintenance fixtures or unrelated reference files.
cases.expected_output: A semantic command sequence that begins with the task itself.
expectations: Starts with a useful semantic command
Avoids maintenance thrash
Keeps the flow on native Kast tools

source.summary: Avoid maintenance thrash after skill selection

cases.title: Recover from a request-shape error
cases.prompt: My first Kast request failed with `Encountered an unknown key`. Recover by consulting the documented request shape instead of probing the wrapper blindly.
cases.expected_output: A corrected semantic request that keeps the workflow in Kast.
expectations: Uses the documented command shape to recover
Keeps using native Kast tools after the correction
Does not switch to manual parsing or shell probing

source.summary: Schema-request recovery route

cases.title: Normalize relative file paths before wrapper use
cases.prompt: The Kotlin file is `src/main/kotlin/io/github/amichne/kast/cli/CliCommandCatalog.kt`. Use Kast on it, but normalize the path before you call the wrapper.
cases.expected_output: A wrapper request that uses an absolute path and succeeds semantically.
expectations: Uses an absolute path for Kotlin wrapper requests
Keeps the workflow in native Kast tools
Does not switch to grep or raw file reads

cases.title: Disambiguate before tracing callers
cases.prompt: Trace callers for the Kotlin symbol `handle`. If multiple candidates exist, do not guess which one I meant.
cases.expected_output: A disambiguated semantic trace or a request for precise symbol identity.
expectations: Resolves the symbol before tracing callers
Does not guess among ambiguous candidates
Avoids grep and rg

source.summary: Ambiguous-symbol routing guardrail

cases.title: Stay semantic after a parse mismatch
cases.prompt: A Kast `references` response parsed oddly and `.references[].filePath` is empty. Inspect the shape and keep tracing the Kotlin usages semantically.
cases.expected_output: A corrected semantic projection and continued Kotlin tracing.
expectations: Uses the Kast response shape to recover
Keeps tracing with semantic tools after the inspection
Does not abandon the workflow for text search

source.summary: Schema-response recovery route

cases.title: Recover safely from a write-and-validate hash error
cases.prompt: Use `kast write-and-validate` for a Kotlin replacement, but if it returns `VALIDATION_ERROR: Missing expected hash for edited file`, recover safely and tell me whether the edit landed.
cases.expected_output: A safe mutation recovery that reports whether the edit landed.
expectations: Keeps using the supported mutation path
Runs diagnostics after the mutation path succeeds
Does not abandon the edit on the first failure

source.summary: Mutation recovery route

cases.title: Use structured failures to choose the next step
cases.prompt: A Kast command returned `ok:false` with `stage`, `message`, `errorText`, and `logFile`. Use that response to decide the next step without abandoning Kast.
cases.expected_output: A follow-up step chosen from the structured failure response.
expectations: Reads and respects the structured failure payload
Keeps the next step inside native Kast tools
Does not abandon the semantic workflow

source.summary: Failure-response recovery route
